evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: well documented and well govern; standards and guidelines; governance; best practices
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour dominates: governance, documentation, best practices, and standards-setting are core responsibilities with no velocity signals present.
evidence.domain_risk: internal analytics; business functions; data platform
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Internal data infrastructure serving business analytics without financial/compliance/regulatory framing indicates low risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineers; Data Analysts; Data Scientists; different business functions
evidence.data_team_maturity: join an established team; Data Platform team; developing more junior team members; implementing best practices
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Multiple named data roles (DE, DA, DS) with established platform and mentorship responsibilities indicate mature organization.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build-up our Data Warehouse by developing robust and efficient data pipelines and data models; Ensure our data is well documented and well govern; Setting up proper models in our BI tools and AI assistants
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical responsibilities (dbt, Snowflake, Airflow) and named stack elements show hiring manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: data-ready for end-users; BI tools; AI assistants; third party products
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary focus is enabling internal data consumers (analysts, end-users) and downstream AI systems via internal data infrastructure.
evidence.autonomy_level: setting up standards and guidelines; participating at setting up the architecture direction; key role in developing junior team members
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic ownership language (setting up direction, guidelines, architecture) demonstrates role with priority-setting authority.
evidence.ai_role: Make our data ready to use by our end-users by setting up proper models in our BI tools and AI assistants; Support the use of our data by third party products
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Candidate builds data infrastructure that AI assistants consume—clear ai_enabler signal.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: dbt is mentioned in tech stack but no ownership verb or explicit testing responsibility; appears as tool available, not owned practice.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: governance and reliability are concerns but secondary to delivery; not compliance/trust-driven.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build-up our Data Warehouse; consolidating existing ones; Improve how we work
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of building new assets and improving/consolidating existing infrastructure.
